titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to Yale University Buildings and Grounds Architectural Documentation
Yale University Buildings and Grounds Architectural Documentation
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 13, 2020 at 10:28

did.unittitle: Yale University buildings and grounds architectural documentation
did.abstract: The materials consist of correspondence, memoranda, specifications, articles, reports, sketches, photographs, floor plans, notes, booklets, and related materials documenting the planning, construction, renovation, and use of Yale buildings.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 46-A (YRG 46-A), Records and materials broadly documenting Yale buildings, facilities, and grounds.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The materials have come from a variety of sources, including transfers from the Yale offices concerned with building design, construction, and maintenance; other Yale offices; and individual donors. Additional materials concerning Yale buildings and facilities that do not form an integral body of records on their own may be added to this collection.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Yale University Buildings and Grounds Architectural Documentation (RU 2). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Yale University records are arranged and described at the accession level by the creating office. The University Archives creates collection level descriptive records, but typically does no further arrangement and description at the accession level.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The materials consist of correspondence, memoranda, specifications, articles, reports, sketches, photographs, floor plans, notes, booklets, and related materials documenting the planning, construction, renovation, and use of Yale buildings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are arranged in two series: I. General documentation, 1906-1974; II. Documentation on specific buildings, 1747-2006; and subsequent accessions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes tender of buildings, equipment, instructions, and news articles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Old Boat House, Gale's Ferry, and Bob Cook.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes faculty directories, floor plans and news articles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes regulations and floor plans.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes building descriptions and floor plans.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Nathan Smith Hall, Quonset huts on Whitney Avenue, and housing on Hillhouse Avenue, Lake Place, Howard Street, Temple Avenue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes rate lists and floor plans.

did.unittitle: Kline Science Center - construction and renovations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes printed copy of John W. Sterling's will.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pictures, drawings, floor plans, and news articles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pictures, articles, and other documentation on buildings occupied by the school.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes news articles, pictures, and building measurements.

did.unittitle: Yale-New Haven Hospital and Yale Medical School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pictures, cost sheets, floor plans, and documents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes walking tour information, news clippings, brochures, newsletters, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes news clippings, brochures, newsletters, Yale Alumni Magazine articles, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged by building (region:chronology) code.

did.unittitle: Two photostats regarding the 1747 lottery for the building

News articles, pictures, documentation, and cornerstone data
Addition to University Library - minutes, drawings, and specifications
General description of alterations and specifications for water closets, carriage shed, plumbing, and gas fitting; H.W. Lindsey, architect
Specifications for slaters, plumbing, gas, fitting, masons, furniture, and steam fitting; H.W. Lindsey, architect
News articles, pictures, and floor plans
Floor plans, cost sheets, and pictures

Papers and drawings for transformer vault

scopecontent.p: Includes extensive articles on Memorial Quadrangle.

Clippings on the opening of Gala Italiana
Pictures, floor plans, and news articles

scopecontent.p: Includes 1948 Chapel St. Tenants.

scopecontent.p: Includes heating system information.

Correspondence from Remington Rand regarding the stack layout and wire mesh partitions

Manuscripts published to commemorate the dedication of the library
News articles, press releases, and floor plans

scopecontent.p: Includes Wilson Library Bulletin , 1963.

American Institute of Architects Honor Award to Skidmore, Owings, and Merrill
Pictures, news articles, and floor plans

scopecontent.p: Includes background data on Sheffield School and Vanderbilt Hall.

scopecontent.p: Includes floor plans.

did.unittitle: Planning documents for Mason and Dunham Laboratories alterations and Becton construction and renovation

scopecontent.p: Includes Yale vs. Dartmouth booklet, 1959 Feb 21.

did.unittitle: Correspondence and lists regarding the dedication of Sterling Chemistry Laboratory
Pictures, floor plans, news articles, and documentation
Letters from invitees unable to attend the dedication
Letters accepting invitation to speak at the Dedication
Correspondence regarding the American Chemical Society meeting
ASolid Solutions, a paper given by Guiseppe Bruni
"Twenty-Five Years of Research on the Yttrium Earths" (two copies: French and English), paper by G. Urbain
Plan by R.H. Ferguson, Jr.; clippings
Records regarding the building of the Oceanography and Ornithology Building, Douglas Orr, architect
Pictures, floor plans, news articles, and documentation
Specifications, charts, construction and installation documentation
Specifications for carpenter's and mason's work and materials, L.W. Robinson, architect
Specifications for plumbing fixtures, Crane Co., Grosvenor Atterbury, architects
Broad Street extension - skeleton specification, Charles Z. Klauder, architect
Extension - specifications for heating, ventilation, and piping by Hollis French and Allen Hubbard, consulting engineers
Library extension - schedule of hardware for Dwight Building Company
Crane Co. plumbing fixture book, comparison of cubic feet with Presbyterian, Beth Israel, and Fifth Ave. Hospitals
Plumbing fixture information from Crane Company, Henry C. Pelton, architect
Specifications for electrical fixtures and sterilizing equipment, Henry C. Pelton, architect
Specifications for the completion of Bethany Observatory, Hilldale Rd., Bethany, CT

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Yale Bowl and other Derby Ave. athletic buildings.

scopecontent.p: Includes house floor plan.

did.unittitle: Specifications from the Office of Douglas Orr, architect, and the Austin Co.

460 Prospect St. - residence of Irving Fisher: specifications for changes, Angell and Swift, Architects
250 Church St. - Woolsey House: copy of letters to John F. Stevens from Carl A. Lohmann regarding "Certain Pieces"
1:2 Connecticut Hall, National Historic Landmark Certificate
Elevations, floor plans, copies of construction contract and related papers, correspondence from Talbot F. Hamlin to Hollon A. Farr regarding construction information
Floor plans, elevations, and shelving information from libraries of Harvard University, University of Michigan, University of Minnesota, Williams College
Schemes, proposals, charts, floor plans and notes; photographs of building during construction and after completion
13:18 Becton Engineering and Applied Science Center
Architect's report, drawings of buildings to be removed, chart for departmental planning
News clippings, preservation newsletters, and other related historical information
"What Should We Do With Woolsey Hall?" by Thomas C. Duffy, Assoc. Professor, Yale School of Music
"What Should We Do With Woolsey Hall?" by Thomas C. Duffy, Assoc. Professor, Yale School of Music
